<!-- Chart CH-TAB-02 HEATMAP TABLE, role-based (themeable) -->

## HEATMAP TABLE chart (CH-TAB-02)

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Colors are ROLES, not hex: resolve each role against the active design
language token block (a DL-xx block pasted next to this one). A role
chain like `benchmark -> muted` means use the first role the palette
defines.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-TAB-02",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "status",
    "lookup",
    "comparison"
  ],
  "form": "entities x metrics table, cells shaded per column and numbered",
  "data_required": "entity rows x numeric metric columns with different scales",
  "family": "TAB",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-TAB-01 (status enums instead of numbers)",
    "CH-COR-03 (two ordered dimensions)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "cells are statuses, not numbers",
      "use": "CH-TAB-01"
    },
    {
      "when": "both dimensions are ordered",
      "use": "CH-COR-03"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- entities compared across metrics with different scales (progress %, punch count, tests left)
- the reader looks up their row but the pattern should jump out

Do NOT use when:
- one shared scale across all columns (use CH-COR-03's global mode)
- status enums (CH-TAB-01)
- more than 8 rows or 6 metric columns

### Input data shape
```json
{
  "title": "string, action title naming the row that stands out",
  "eyebrow": "string caps, rendered only when the DL chrome asks for it",
  "source": "string, rendered bottom-left, always",
  "columns": "metric names, caps",
  "rows": "array of {label, values[] same length as columns}"
}
```

### Exact layout (px on the canvas, follow literally)
```json
{
  "canvas": {
    "width": 960,
    "height": 540
  },
  "layout": {
    "title": {
      "x": 32,
      "y": 56,
      "y_with_eyebrow": 88,
      "size": 24
    },
    "kicker": {
      "x": 32,
      "y": 28,
      "w": 24,
      "h": 4
    },
    "eyebrow": {
      "x": 32,
      "y": 52,
      "size": 11,
      "letter_spacing": 2
    },
    "source": {
      "x": 32,
      "y": 516,
      "size": 11
    },
    "plot": {
      "left": 200,
      "right": 928,
      "top": 168
    },
    "row_height": 48,
    "header_label": {
      "size": 10,
      "offset": 14
    },
    "row_label": {
      "size": 12,
      "offset": 16
    },
    "value_label": {
      "size": 11
    }
  },
  "scale": {
    "normalize": "column",
    "note": "each column normalized to its own max: intensity means 'high for this metric', not absolute size"
  }
}
```

### Rules
- per-column normalization; the spec's one job is making mixed scales comparable
- intensity encodes magnitude, not judgment: the title says whether high is good or bad
- values printed in every cell
- rows in the reader's natural order, never sorted by health
- single-hue ramp

### Never do this
- red-green judgment coloring on magnitude data
- global normalization across mixed units
- cells without printed values
- sorting rows by intensity

### QA before delivering
- [ ] per-column normalization verified
- [ ] every cell prints its value
- [ ] rows in natural order
- [ ] title names the standout row with a number
- [ ] source line present bottom-left

### Color roles

| Element | Role chain |
|---|---|
| background | background |
| cell_low | panel -> background |
| cell_high | primary |
| header | body |
| row_label | ink |
| value | body |
| title | ink |
| source | body |

### Fonts

Title uses the token block's `display` font; everything else uses
`body`. Weight 600 only on highlighted items.
